worldgriddeployer.mode.description: Places into crossed cells of the stationary world
worldgriddeployer.config.title: Create World-Grid Deployer
worldgriddeployer.config.subtitle: Configuration and diagnostics
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.tooltip: Configure local geometry and server-authoritative placement overlays for this connection.
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.offline.t: Configure client viewing preferences without joining a world. Local histories begin fresh on join; authoritative outcomes still require a compatible server and its permission policy.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.tooltip: Inspect the outcome-stream policy and, with permission level 3, edit it safely on the server.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.private_: A private single-player world has no remote players to authorize. Open it to LAN to expose these controls.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.unsuppor: This server did not negotiate the World-Grid settings channel. Its server access policy cannot be viewed or changed from this client.
worldgriddeployer.config.world_required: Join a world or server before configuring connection-scoped debugging.
worldgriddeployer.config.cancel.tooltip: Discard staged changes and return to the previous screen.
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.title: World-Grid Debug Overlays
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.subtitle: Connection-scoped histories; maximum %s seconds
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.targets.t: Draws each deployer's exact working point and candidate world block through terrain. Green means locally powered; red means locally unpowered.
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.point_pat: Draws a fading cyan line through the precise floating-point target samples. Breaks mark inactivity, teleports, or missing samples.
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.block_tra: Draws fading blue boxes for every world cell the client traversal predicts could be visited. It does not prove placement succeeded.
Requests actual server placement results. Availability follows the server policy; denied requests remain pending while connected.
Stages the viewing preference now, then requests actual placement results after joining. A server without this mod or without permission simply provides no authoritative outcome data.
This server did not negotiate the optional outcome channel. The preference remains visible, but no request is sent and no authoritative result boxes are available here.
Click to cycle how long this category remains visible. Changing it starts a fresh history for that category.
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.all_on.to: Stages every overlay as enabled. Press Done to apply.
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.all_off.t: Stages every overlay as disabled. Press Done to apply and clear their histories.
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.clear.too: Immediately discards all collected points, blocks, live targets, and outcomes without changing which categories are enabled.
worldgriddeployer.config.debug.save.tool: Apply these client-local settings. Server Outcomes also updates the current connection's subscription.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.title: Server Outcome Access
Integrated LAN server — authority is still checked server-side
Remote server — read-only without permission level 3
Waiting for the server's authoritative settings snapshot.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.locked.t: Read-only: the server did not grant this player command permission level 3.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.policy.t: Controls only the authoritative outcome stream. Local target, path, and candidate-block overlays remain client-side.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.policy.c: Click to cycle; the change remains staged until Save.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.policy.o: Only server operators receive outcomes. This is the default.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.policy.w: Operators and UUIDs in the list receive outcomes. The list is preserved under other policies.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.policy.p: Every connected client with this mod may receive outcomes after opting in.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.username: Enter an online or previously cached username. The server resolves it to a UUID only when you Save.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.add.tool: Adds this name to the staged edit. Nothing changes on the server until Save succeeds.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.autocomp: Online and already-listed names autocomplete. Type a prefix and press Tab; Enter stages it. Other cached names may still be entered in full.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.remove.t: Click to stage removal. Save applies all additions, removals, and the policy atomically.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.page.too: Move through the staged whitelist without changing it.
Discard staged edits and request the current authoritative settings from the server.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.save.too: Send one revision-checked, atomic edit. The server rechecks permission and resolves every staged username before changing anything.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.save.no_: No policy or whitelist changes are currently staged.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.entry.pe: %s will be resolved by the server when saved.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.result.s: Settings changed elsewhere; the latest server state was reloaded.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.result.n: The server refused the edit because permission is no longer sufficient.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.result.u: The server could not resolve player ‘%s’; no changes were applied.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.result.i: The server rejected invalid settings (%s); no changes were applied.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.result.e: Enter a username before staging it.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.result.d: %s is already in the staged whitelist.
worldgriddeployer.config.access.result.s: %s is staged; press Save to resolve and apply it.
